data-cleanup-task.label: Data Cleanup Task
data-cleanup-task.summary: Use string functions and filtering to clean up data.

sketch-intro-cleanup.title: Why Data Cleanup Matters
sketch-intro-cleanup.bodyMarkdown: Real-world data is often messy: names may have extra spaces, inconsistent capitalization, or unwanted characters. Cleaning and standardizing data using SQL functions helps ensure accuracy and makes analysis easier.

sketch-sql-cleanup-tools.title: SQL Tools for Data Cleanup
sketch-sql-cleanup-tools.bodyMarkdown:
Common SQL string functions for cleaning data include:
- `TRIM()` to remove extra spaces
- `UPPER()` or `LOWER()` to standardize case
- `REPLACE()` to substitute unwanted characters
- Filtering with `WHERE` to exclude bad data
These tools help you prepare your data for reliable analysis.

quiz-trim-function.title: Removing Extra Spaces
quiz-trim-function.prompt: Which SQL function removes leading and trailing spaces from a text column?
quiz-trim-function.hint: Think about a function that targets spaces at the start and end.

help.concept: The TRIM() function is used to remove unwanted spaces from the beginning and end of a string.
help.hint_1: It's not REPLACE(), which substitutes specific characters.
help.hint_2: Look for a function that specifically targets whitespace at the edges of text.

quiz-standardize-case.title: Standardizing Text Case
quiz-standardize-case.prompt: Which SQL functions can you use to make all letters in a column uppercase or lowercase?
quiz-standardize-case.hint: There are two functions, one for each case.

help.concept: UPPER() converts text to all uppercase letters, while LOWER() converts text to all lowercase.
help.hint_1: TRIM() and REPLACE() do not change letter case.
help.hint_2: Both UPPER() and LOWER() are used for standardizing capitalization.

quiz-replace-character.title: Replacing Characters
quiz-replace-character.prompt: Fill in the blank: To replace all dashes '-' with spaces in a column, use _____.
quiz-replace-character.hint: What do you want the dash to become?

help.concept: The REPLACE() function substitutes all occurrences of a substring with another string.
help.hint_1: You want to turn dashes into spaces.
help.hint_2: The replacement string should be a single space character.

quiz-cleanup-sql-task.title: Clean Up Customer Names
quiz-cleanup-sql-task.prompt: Write a SQL query to select the first_name and last_name columns from the customers_cleanup table, but with all leading/trailing spaces removed and all letters in uppercase. Name the columns as cleaned_first and cleaned_last.
quiz-cleanup-sql-task.hint: Use both TRIM() and UPPER() on each name column.

help.concept: Combine string functions to clean and standardize text in SQL queries. Use TRIM() to remove spaces and UPPER() to convert to uppercase.
help.hint_1: Wrap TRIM() inside UPPER() for each column.
help.hint_2: Alias the cleaned columns as cleaned_first and cleaned_last.
